title: Hello world — starting the lab notebook
description: A first post: why this site exists, what I'll be writing about, and what to expect.
bodyMd:
## Why this exists

I've spent the last six years building software for other people — first at Lipsey's, then at Tri-Core — while shipping my own projects on the side. *CookJunkie*, *TutorTab*, *Vorbiz*, and *Weekend Plant* all started because I wanted to see what I could build on my own, end to end, without training wheels.

This blog is the notebook where I write about that work.

## What I'll write about

- **Bringing old systems forward.** Taking software that still works but is starting to show its age, and modernizing it without the business skipping a beat.
- **Using AI to do real work.** Not chatbot demos — AI that actually reviews data, drafts content, and handles the repetitive tasks that used to eat my afternoons.
- **Building and running software on my own.** The unglamorous parts: payments that go sideways, calendars that silently drop appointments, customers who need the app to work on a dead connection.
- **Short notes on things I learn.** No filler, no content marketing.

## What to expect

Some posts will be written. Some will be short videos. Some will be both — watch or read, whichever you prefer.

## What I'm working on right now

This site, actually. The portfolio and the blog itself — it felt like the right place to start.

If any of this sounds like the kind of work you need, there's a [contact form](/contact). Tell me what you're working on.
